zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster於1160年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，他的父親Walter de Burgh27歲，母親Alice de Bourgogne18歲。他於大約1194年與Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1199年登記服兵役。他於1201年被列為seneschal (royal governor) of munster 。他去世於1206年2月之前在愛爾蘭，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 迪拉利塞西爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster于1160年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，他的父亲Walter de Burgh27岁，母亲Alice de Bourgogne18岁。他于大约1194年与Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1199年登记服兵役。他于1201年被列为seneschal (royal governor) of munster 。他去世于1206年2月之前在爱尔兰，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 迪拉利塞西爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster was born in 1160, in Ulster, Ireland, his father, Walter de Burgh, was 27 and his mother, Alice de Bourgogne, was 18. He married Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain about 1194, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1199. In 1201, his occupation is listed as seneschal (royal governor) of munster . He died before February 1206, in Ireland, and was buried in Golden, County Tipperary, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster est né en 1160, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Walter de Burgh, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice de Bourgogne, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain environ 1194, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1199. En 1201, sa profession de son indiquée est seneschal (royal governor) of munster . Il est décédé avant février 1206, à Irlande, et a été enterré à Golden, County Tipperary, Irlande.

de.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster 1160 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Burgh 27 und seine Mutter Alice de Bourgogne 18. Er hat um 1194 in Irland Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1199 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1201 ist sein Beruf als seneschal (royal governor) of munster  aufgeführt. Er starb vor Februar 1206 in Irland und wurde in Golden, County Tipperary, Irland begraben.

Lui sposato Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain circa 1194, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1199. Nel 1201, la sua occupazione è indicata come seneschal (royal governor) of munster . Morì prima del febbraio 1206, nel Irlanda, e fu sepolto a Golden, County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Burgh Seneschal of Munsterが1160年にアイルランドウルスターで生まれた時，父Walter de Burghは27歳，母Alice de Bourgogneは18歳であった。約1194年にアイルランドでEilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1199年に兵役登録をした。1201年，職業はseneschal (royal governor) of munster と記録されている。彼は1206年2月以前にアイルランドで死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ティップエラリーゴールデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster(이)가 1160년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Burgh(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Bourgogne(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1194년 아일랜드에서 Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1199년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1201년, 그의 직업은 seneschal (royal governor) of munster 이었다. 그는 1206년 2월 이전 아일랜드에서 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 티페라리 골든에 묻혔다.

pt.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster nasceu em 1160, em Ulster, Irlanda, seu pai, Walter de Burgh, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Bourgogne, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain aproximadamente 1194, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1199. Em 1201, sua profissão aparece como seneschal (royal governor) of munster . Ele faleceu antes fevereiro de 1206, em Irlanda, e foi sepultado em Golden, County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ru.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster родился в 1160 г. в местности улстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Walter de Burgh было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Bourgogne, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain около 1194 г. в местности Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1199 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1201 годуработал по профессии seneschal (royal governor) of munster . Он умер перед февраль 1206 г. в местности Ирландия и был похоронен в местности голден, коунти типпирэри, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster nació en 1160, en Ulster, Irlanda, su papá, Walter de Burgh, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alice de Bourgogne, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain aproximadamente 1194, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1199. En 1201, su ocupación registrada era seneschal (royal governor) of munster . Murió antes de febrero de 1206, en Irlanda, y fue sepultado en Golden, County Tipperary, Irlanda.
